definitions.gloss_en: a morale or discipline stat representing a unit's willingness to keep fighting under pressure
definitions.gloss_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}が{苦|く}{境|きょう}でも{戦|たたか}い{続|つづ}けようとする{意|い}{志|し}・{規|き}{律|りつ}を{示|しめ}す{ステータス|すてーたす}
definitions.explanation_en: A stat or check representing unit morale. Units that fail discipline checks may flee, become disrupted, or suffer penalties. Wargame equivalent of the military concept of unit cohesion.
definitions.implicit_notes: 士気 is a classical Japanese military concept directly translating the military idea of troop morale or esprit de corps. Used in JWC documentation consistently.
definitions.orthography_notes: 士気: 士 (warrior/soldier) + 気 (spirit/energy). Classical Japanese military term for morale. Goo dictionary. JWC wargame glossary attests 士気 as the standard morale term. WH Japan uses 戦意フェイズ (battle-spirit phase) for the morale phase — 士気 is the underlying concept and not a WH term.
definitions.game_setting_notes: Standard in board wargames and historical miniature wargames. JWC documentation uses 士気 for morale checks. WH Japan's 戦意フェイズ is a WH-specific phase label; 士気 itself is classical and independent.

usage_jp: {士|し}{気|き}チェック (morale check); {士|し}{気|き}が{崩|くず}れる (morale breaks); {士|し}{気|き}テスト (discipline test)
comparative_notes: 戦意フェイズ (WH Japan) uses 戦意 (battle will) as the morale concept; 士気 is the classical general term. 士気 is safe for all non-WH uses.

examples.japanese: でも、それで{士|し}{気|き}が{下|さ}がっては{本|ほん}{末|まつ}{転|てん}{倒|とう}ではないかと。
examples.english: But, if by that, morale drops, wouldn't that defeat the purpose?

cross_references.note: 交戦中 — engaged in combat; a state that often triggers 士気 checks
